name: Mint-Coriander Chutney
recipeInstructions:
This spicy chutney from India pairs well with party snacks, breads, and rice dishes. Adjust the spiciness to your liking.
DIRECTIONS: 1. Coarsely chop coriander, mint, and peppers. 2. Process the first eight ingredients in a blender, adding enough water to form a coarse paste. Transfer to a nonmetallic container. 3. Heat oil in a saucepan. Add mustard seeds and let them sizzle. Add cumin seeds and stir until aromatic. Remove from heat and add to chutney. Mix well before serving. The chutney is best when made just before serving, but may be refrigerated for up to 24 hours.
